channel.title: NBC Sports Fantasy Football News
channel.description: Latest player news and analysis for fantasy football from NBC Sports.

item.title: Raiders signed No. 1 overall pick Fernando Mendoza to a four-year, $57.3 million contract.
item.description: The Raiders and Mendoza were reportedly at a standstill over how his signing bonus would be paid out. Mendoza wanted it to be a lump sum, while the Raiders wanted to pay it in installments. Regardless of that minor detail, the value of the contract and that it is fully guaranteed were both set in stone the moment Vegas selected him. The deal also includes a fifth-year team option.-Kyle Dvorchak

item.title: ESPN’s Daniel Oyefusi did not project Cedric Tillman to make the Browns’ 53-man roster.
item.description: Tillman posted a few big games in 2024 and garnered some breakout hype heading into 2025. He struggled to find much of a role, let alone put up decent counting stats, amid another lost season for the Browns. He has been rumored to be a trade candidate heading into his fourth year, but it seems unlikely that any team takes a shot on him at this point. Isaiah Bond is ahead of him on the depth chart and Tillman doesn’t play special teams, making it hard for the Browns to justify keeping him around.-Kyle Dvorchak

item.title: NFL Network’s Cameron Wolfe reports Panthers EDGE Nic Scourton “suffered an apparent lower body injury in practice” and was carted off the field.
item.description: Per Wolfe, Scourton was in tears as he left the field and the entire team gathered around the cart before he exited practice. Scourton will undergo testing for the injury, but the optics are unfortunately concerning. A 2025 second-round pick, Scourton totaled five sacks across 17 games, eight of which were credited as starts. He was set for a larger role this year, but that may have to wait.-Kyle Dvorchak

item.title: Jaguars coach Liam Coen said, “He will do it,” when asked if Travis Hunter would play offense and defense in 2026.
item.description: Talking with Peter Schrager on his podcast, Coen almost laughed at the idea that Hunter might play exclusively on one side of the ball this year. Early offseason reports suggested Hunter might play primarily on defense after suffering a season-ending injury during his rookie season. Every subsequent report from local beats and all the quotes from the coaching staff have made it clear that Hunter remains a two-way player. Coen did note that they are still determining how much he will play on both sides of the ball, but it sounds like a part-time role on offense is the floor. Given the barrage of information suggesting Hunter will play on offense, a WR57 price tag for last year’s No. 2 overall continues to look absurdly cheap.-Kyle Dvorchak

item.title: Eagles coach Nick Sirianni said he is “excited for the extra opportunities” DeVonta Smith will see this year.
item.description: It’s no surprise that Smith is expected to see more work in 2026. The team traded A.J. Brown to New England in the offseason and Makai Lemon won’t replace all of his vacated production as a rookie. All reports from the offseason have suggested that the Eagles believe Smith is ready to be a true No. 1 receiver, including these quotes from Sirianni. He noted that every time Smith has seen an uptick in opportunities, he’s taken advantage of them. As the top target-earner on an offense that should rebound this year, Smith has a chance at WR1 numbers if things break his way in 2026.-Kyle Dvorchak

item.title: Jaguars coach Liam Coen said he is expecting “a big year” from Bhayshul Tuten.
item.description: Speaking on The Schrager Hour, Coen talked up Tuten’s ability to create yards after contact and his straight-line speed. Coen also noted Tuten’s prowess as a red zone runner. Tuten is the presumed starter with Travis Etienne gone, but Chris Rodriguez should still see plenty of work. Coen also coached Rodriguez at Kentucky, giving him some extra familiarity with the offense. Tuten is the higher-upside bet, and early drafters are treating him as such, selecting him as the RB25 compared to RB42 for Rodriguez. It’s likely that one of these two backs pays off their ADP, but it will be hard for both to return value in the same season.-Kyle Dvorchak

item.title: Tyreek Hill said he has “no power” in his left leg.
item.description: Hill suffered a dislocated knee, torn ACL and LCL, and other ligament damage in Week 4 of the 2025 season. He posted a video of himself working out and said he didn’t have a timeline to return, but added that the timeline is “looking real good right now.” Still, the fact that he has no power in his recovering leg speaks louder than his words. Hill is currently a free agent and is likely several months away from playing if things go well. It’s also unlikely we know where he is playing until deep into the season. Fantasy managers, in turn, shouldn’t worry about him in summer drafts.-Kyle Dvorchak

item.title: NBC Houston’s Aaron Wilson reports Joe Mixon told former teammates from the Bengals “his career is over.”
item.description: Wilson did note that a source close to Mixon told him that there is “some hope”, though it doesn’t sound awfully likely that Mixon ever steps on a football field again. Mixon was unable to play last year after injuring his foot and undergoing surgery on his own. The Texans seemed to be in the dark throughout the process and were never able to give a clear answer on when or if Mixon would return. They went on to cut him after he missed the entire 2025 season. Per Wilson, Mixon needed another surgery this offseason. Even if he does somehow return to action, there has been no indication that it will happen this year.-Kyle Dvorchak

item.title: Cardinals TE Travis Vokolek retired after three seasons in the NFL.
item.description: Vokolek announces his retirement on social media Wednesday, noting a neck injury as the cause. The Cardinals tight end was carted off in Week 6 of the 2025 season. The neck injury was serious enough for doctors to advise Vokolek to “medically retire from the NFL.” Coming out of Nebraska as an undrafted free agent in 2023, Vokolek signed with the Ravens’ practice squad. The Cardinals signed him from there and he appeared in eight games over the course of three seasons in Arizona. Primarily a special teamer and blocker, Vokolek’s career ends at 28-years-old.-Aditya Fuldeore

item.title: NFL Network’s Mike Garafolo reports QB Jimmy Garoppolo will play football this season for “the right situation.”
item.description: Garafolo adds that Garoppolo “has not decided to retire” and will continue to play. Nothing is imminent and the “right situation” may take an injury to a starter around the league. The 34-year-old had contemplated retirement earlier in the offseason but instead is open to continuing his playing career. Garoppolo is a free agent to keep an eye on as training camp battles and injuries start to unfold.-Aditya Fuldeore
